skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Guides assistants in drafting internal company communications, including 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Draft clear internal company updates using structured formats for teams, leaders, and employees.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, hardcoded URL, and shell execution patterns, but review found prose-only markdown and license text. No executable code, command invocation, credential handling, or network request implementation was found. The only residual concern is that the skill may summarize sensitive internal sources when a user grants access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings are keyword collisions in natural-language guidance and Apache license text. There is no hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation words and license clauses only. No code path performs cryptographic operations or selects a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Writing Guidance
low_findings.description: The duplicate reconnaissance findings point to a sentence about using the word "we" in company newsletters. This is style guidance, not host inspection, environment discovery, or system inventory behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is about editorial voice for newsletters. There are no commands or APIs that inspect the local system.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are standard Apache License references. They are not called by code and do not create network traffic or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs appear inside the standard license text. No executable component reads or requests them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are inline markdown file paths in SKILL.md. They identify guideline files and are not executable backtick expressions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit file names in a markdown list. There is no Ruby, shell script, or command interpreter context.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Internal Source Aggregation Requires User Control
low_findings.description: The skill asks assistants to use Slack, email, calendar, documents, and press references when available. This is legitimate for internal communications, but users should grant only necessary data access and review summaries before sharing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited newsletter guidance clearly requests review of internal sources, which can include sensitive content. Risk depends on user-granted connector access and sharing controls.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often lose clarity when teams use different formats and levels of detail. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft concise company communications from structured guidance.
actual_capabilities: Selects a communication format based on the requested update type.
Drafts 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Creates company newsletter outlines with concise sectioned bullet points.
Turns common employee questions into short FAQ answers.
Requests audience, purpose, tone, and format details for custom communications.
Encourages links to authoritative internal sources when the user provides access.
limitations: Does not include scripts, integrations, or automatic connector setup.
Cannot verify Slack, email, calendar, or document content without user-granted access.
Does not decide confidential messaging policy or legal approvals.
Requires user review before broad company distribution.

use_cases.title: Weekly Team 3P Update
use_cases.description: Create a short Progress, Plans, and Problems update for leadership review.

use_cases.title: Company Newsletter Draft
use_cases.description: Summarize major announcements, milestones, and leadership messages for all employees.

use_cases.title: Employee FAQ Response
use_cases.description: Answer repeated employee questions using clear, source-backed language.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with source notes already provided.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update for the Platform team covering this week. Use these notes and keep it under one minute to read: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Newsletter
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a company-wide summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a company newsletter for the past two weeks. Group items by theme and include links where I provide them: [paste announcements and links].

prompt_templates.title: Build an FAQ
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request based on employee confusion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these employee questions into an internal FAQ. Keep each answer to two sentences and mark uncertain items for executive review: [paste questions].

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Multiple Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using several authorized inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a leadership update from these Slack summaries, meeting notes, and documents. Prioritize company-wide impact, metrics, risks, and decisions needed: [paste sources].

output_examples.input: A product team needs a weekly 3P update for executives.
output_examples.output:
🚀 Product Platform (June 22 to June 28)
Progress: Shipped the new onboarding checklist and reduced setup time by 18%.
Plans: Complete rollout monitoring and prepare the next analytics review.
Problems: Two enterprise requests remain blocked on data export approval.

output_examples.input: Employees are asking repeated questions about a new office policy.
output_examples.output:
Question: When does the new office policy begin?
Answer: The policy begins on July 8. Employees should review the official announcement before making schedule changes.

output_examples.input: A communications manager needs a company newsletter section.
output_examples.output:
Company Announcements
- We completed the quarterly planning process and published team priorities.
- We welcomed the new customer success leader this week.
- We shared the updated launch calendar for the next release.

best_practices: Provide source notes, dates, audience, and desired channel before asking for a draft.
Review drafts for confidentiality, accuracy, and approval requirements before sending.
Use links to official announcements, documents, or executive messages when possible.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the assistant to infer confidential strategy from incomplete context.
Do not publish drafts without checking names, metrics, dates, and links.
Do not mix team-only updates into company-wide messages unless they affect most employees.

faq.question: What types of internal communications does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Does it connect to Slack, email, or documents by itself?
faq.answer: No. It only suggests using those sources when the user has already granted access or provided the content.

faq.question: Can it write for executives and employees?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes formats for leadership updates, broad company newsletters, and employee FAQ responses.

faq.question: How should sensitive information be handled?
faq.answer: Users should limit source access, remove confidential details when needed, and review each draft before sharing.

faq.question: Does the skill enforce a strict format?
faq.answer: It enforces a strict 3P structure and gives flexible guidance for newsletters, FAQs, and custom communications.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed package is markdown-only and contains no executable code, network calls, or command execution.
